headline_summary: Iran war-related incidents expand into Gulf states: drones hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi refinery and a power/water plant; U.S. aircraft are downed over Iran/Gulf.
Reuters reports U.S. intelligence sees Iran unlikely to ease its effective Strait of Hormuz blockade soon, sustaining disruption to a key oil chokepoint.
Diplomatic tracks: UK-led talks among ~40 countries explore post-ceasefire coalition options; UN Security Council vote planned on Bahrain-drafted shipping protection resolution with China opposed.
Selective transit/assurances: Philippines says Iran promised safe passage for Philippine-flagged ships; CMA CGM vessel transits Hormuz after signaling 'Owner France'.
Oil market structure reflects acute tightness: Reuters reports record WTI prompt backwardation and elevated prices; OPEC+ weighs further output hike contingent on reopening.
risk_drivers: Effective closure/constraint of the Strait of Hormuz and uncertainty over reopening timeline
Direct kinetic spillover into Gulf infrastructure (refinery and utilities hit in Kuwait)
Air/air-defense incidents involving U.S. aircraft and rescue operations
Potential for Iran to formalize permit/fee regime for transit, keeping insurance/freight elevated
Unclear end-state and ongoing escalation rhetoric

Energy: oil_up_risk: sustained supply shock and higher freight/insurance costs while Hormuz remains constrained
Refined product volatility risk from Gulf refinery disruptions (Kuwait incident)
Backwardation/near-term tightness may persist until credible reopening/escort plan emerges
FX: em_fx_pressure: higher energy import bills pressure EM current accounts
risk_off_flows could support USD and safe havens if Gulf spillover widens
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: higher energy-driven inflation risk can lift term premia while widening credit spreads in risk-off
Event risk may increase rate volatility around geopolitical headlines
Equities: risk_off_flows: broad equity drawdown risk on escalation; relative support for energy/defense vs transports
Supply-chain and fuel cost headwinds for cyclicals

sources.title: Exclusive: US intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease Hormuz Strait chokehold soon, sources say
Drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery, fires reported, no injuries
Britain says 40 countries discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz after Iran blockade
UN to vote on Hormuz resolution as China opposes authorization of force
World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
Near-term oil prices hit record premium over later deliveries after Trump vows more attacks on Iran
OPEC+ likely to weigh further oil output hike on Sunday, sources say

events.title: Iran shoots down U.S. F-15E over southwestern Iran; one crew member missing
events.implication: Downing of U.S. aircraft raises escalation risk and could trigger retaliatory strikes impacting Gulf security.

events.title: Iranian fire hits U.S. A-10; aircraft crashes over Kuwait after engagement
events.implication: Cross-border incident highlights spillover into Gulf states and risk to energy infrastructure.

events.title: Iranian fire hits two U.S. Blackhawks during pilot search; helicopters exit Iranian airspace
events.implication: Search-and-rescue operations under fire increase likelihood of broader engagement and miscalculation.

events.title: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
events.implication: Direct hit on refining assets elevates risk premium in refined products and regional supply reliability.

events.title: Iran strikes power and water plant in Kuwait, Reuters reports
events.implication: Attacks on critical infrastructure in Gulf states increase tail-risk to energy exports and domestic stability.

events.title: Drone hits Red Crescent relief warehouse in Choghadak, Bushehr province
events.implication: Signals ongoing drone activity and air-defense strain; raises uncertainty around southern Iranian coastal security.

events.title: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
events.implication: Prolonged disruption risk to ~20% of global oil transit supports elevated crude risk premium.

events.title: Iran uses attacks, mines and passage fees to effectively block Hormuz transit, Reuters reports
events.implication: Reduced insurability and throughput implies supply shock and higher freight/insurance costs.

events.title: Philippines says Iran assures safe passage for Philippine-flagged vessels and Filipino seafarers
events.implication: Selective assurances suggest Iran may calibrate pressure, potentially differentiating shipping risk by flag/state.

events.title: CMA CGM-owned container ship Kribi transits Strait of Hormuz; first French-owned vessel since war began
events.implication: A successful transit hints at potential de-escalation channels or negotiated exceptions; still high uncertainty.

events.title: Vessel changes AIS destination to 'Owner France' before entering Iranian waters, Reuters reports
events.implication: Operational tactics to signal neutrality may proliferate, but also indicate heightened threat environment.

events.title: Britain hosts talks with ~40 countries on coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Coalition planning signals potential future escort/mine-clear operations; near-term risk remains high pending ceasefire.

events.title: UK to host meeting of military planners next week to discuss mine-clearing and reassurance force
events.implication: Planning for mine-countermeasures underscores expectation of a contested maritime environment.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect shipping in and around Hormuz
events.implication: Potential authorization for defensive measures affects legal basis for escorts; market sensitive to passage restoration prospects.

events.title: China opposes UNSC language authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' in Hormuz
events.implication: Major-power disagreement reduces probability of a robust UN-mandated maritime mission.

events.title: Iran says it is drafting a protocol with Oman requiring ship permits and licenses for Hormuz transit
events.implication: Institutionalizing control over transit can function as de facto restriction and keep insurance/freight elevated.

events.title: Iranian military spokesperson says Hormuz closed 'long term' to U.S. and Israel
events.implication: Explicit closure stance sustains crude supply shock and elevates geopolitical risk premia.

events.title: EU foreign policy chief says international law doesn't recognize 'pay-to-pass' schemes
events.implication: Signals pushback against Iran-controlled fee regime; diplomatic tensions may prolong disruption.

events.title: Trump says U.S. will hit Iran 'extremely hard' over next 2-3 weeks; threatens civilian power plants
events.implication: Signals sustained military campaign; markets likely to price higher probability of regional spillover.

events.title: U.S. strike hits Tehran–Karaj B1 bridge; Iran state media reports casualties
events.implication: Attack on civilian infrastructure increases escalation risk and retaliation incentives.

events.title: Iran warns of 'broader and more destructive' attacks in response to U.S. threats
events.implication: Forward guidance of retaliation raises tail-risk for Gulf infrastructure and shipping.

events.title: Iran-linked actors target an Amazon cloud computing centre in Bahrain, per Reuters report of Iranian media
events.implication: Critical digital infrastructure targeting increases operational risk for logistics and energy firms.

events.title: Explosions heard in Damascus; Syrian TV says likely Israeli interception of Iranian missiles
events.implication: Regional air-defense engagements keep escalation risk elevated across Levant air corridors.

events.title: Missile interception debris causes fires near KEZAD; six injured
events.implication: Demonstrates spillover risk to UAE logistics/industrial zones; supports regional risk premia.

events.title: U.S. intelligence can only confirm about one-third of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed, sources say
events.implication: Residual Iranian strike capacity increases duration/uncertainty of conflict and infrastructure risk.

events.title: WTI front-month backwardation hits record as traders scramble for prompt barrels amid Iran war
events.implication: Extreme backwardation signals acute near-term supply tightness and supports oil_up_risk.

events.title: WTI May peaks at $113.97 and settles at $111.42, Reuters reports
events.implication: High prices feed inflation expectations and can pressure risk assets.

events.title: OPEC+ considers further output hike contingent on Hormuz reopening, sources say
events.implication: Policy signaling may cap medium-term prices if reopening occurs; near-term constrained by export routes.

events.title: Saudi exports via Yanbu near capacity as it reroutes crude to bypass Hormuz, Reuters reports
events.implication: Bypass constraints limit ability to fully offset Hormuz disruption; supports elevated crude differentials.

events.title: UAE exports from Fujairah rise as Hormuz bypass route used, Reuters cites Kpler
events.implication: Higher Fujairah flows partially mitigate disruption but may tighten regional condensate supply.

events.title: Russian mariner detained after 2025 Houthi sinking departs Sanaa for Russia after treatment
events.implication: Reminds market of persistent Red Sea security overhang and potential for renewed shipping disruptions.

events.title: Hamas tells mediators it won't discuss disarmament without guarantees of full Israeli withdrawal from Gaza
events.implication: Stalled Gaza disarmament talks can prolong regional tensions and intersect with broader Iran-linked escalation dynamics.

events.title: Macron says military seizure of Hormuz is unrealistic; reopening must be via diplomacy
events.implication: Reduces likelihood of near-term forced reopening; supports sustained oil risk premium.

events.title: Trump calls on Gulf oil-dependent countries and NATO allies to take lead in reopening Hormuz, Reuters reports
events.implication: Burden-shifting could delay effective escort operations, keeping disruption risk elevated.

events.title: Military planners to consider intelligence sharing and air/sea coordination for Hormuz reassurance force
events.implication: Coordination needs underline complexity; any delays maintain higher energy and freight costs.

links.from: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Prolonged constraint on a chokepoint carrying ~20% of global oil trade implies sustained supply risk.

links.from: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
links.why: Direct hit to refining capacity adds outage risk premium to crude/refined markets.

links.from: Iran shoots down U.S. F-15E over southwestern Iran; one crew member missing
links.why: Escalation involving U.S. aircraft raises tail risk and can drive safe-haven flows.

links.from: Britain hosts talks with ~40 countries on coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Coalition planning may lower medium-term disruption risk but depends on ceasefire and execution.

links.from: Iran says it is drafting a protocol with Oman requiring ship permits and licenses for Hormuz transit
links.why: If permits/fees persist, energy/freight inflation can tighten financial conditions and widen spreads.

links.from: WTI front-month backwardation hits record as traders scramble for prompt barrels amid Iran war
links.why: Record prompt premium reflects acute near-term supply tightness.

links.from: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
links.why: Higher refined prices and supply insecurity can worsen import costs for energy-importing EMs.

links.from: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect shipping in and around Hormuz
links.why: Authorization could enable defensive measures, but outcome/implementation uncertain.
